name: Marshmallow Roses and Leaves
recipeIngredient: green and pink paste or liquid food color
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine marshmallows and 2 cups of confectioner's sugar in a food processor and process until large crumbs form. Transfer crumbs to a microwave-safe bowl, then microwave on high for 10 seconds. Work the mixture with your hands, kneading, squeezing, and pulling until a slightly sticky paste forms (about 10 minutes).
2. Add the remaining 1 cup confectioner's sugar, microwaving in 5-10 second increments to soften. The result should be a smooth, elastic dough.
3. Divide dough into two portions; color one green and the other pink using food coloring. Wrap the pink portion in plastic wrap for easier handling.
4. Place a shallow pie plate or container with about half an inch of granulated sugar. This will support and hold leaves and roses while drying.
5. LEAVES: Dust work surface and rolling pin with cornstarch. Roll out green dough to 18x4 inches, trim edges, cut lengthwise into 3/4-inch-wide strips, then diagonally into diamonds. Mark veins on each leaf using the sharp side of a small knife. Twist leaves gently and set in sugar.
6. ROSES: Roll out the pink dough as thinly as possible (keep the rest wrapped). Cut several circles about 3/4 inch in diameter using a piping tube or round cutter. Press scraps into a thick "stem" about 1/2 inch high. Dust fingers with cornstarch and press one circle of dough between fingers until thin, wavy-edged, and about half again as wide. Wrap around the stem, adding petals (10-12) firmly against the stem. Remove stem and place rose in sugar.
Note: When working with hot mixture or sharp tools, handle with care to avoid burns.
